taskDescription: Click on the key anatomical terms related to the eyeball in the text below.
textField:
The *eyeball* has three coats. The outer *fibrous* coat comprises the transparent *cornea* anteriorly and the opaque *sclera* posteriorly. The middle *vascular* coat (*uvea*) consists of the *choroid*, *ciliary* body, and *iris*. The inner *retina* contains *photoreceptors* — *rods* for dim-light vision and *cones* for colour and high-acuity vision.

The *cornea* is the principal refractive structure, supplied by the *ophthalmic* division of the *trigeminal* nerve. The *lens* is suspended by *zonular* fibres from the ciliary body and changes shape during *accommodation*. The *fovea* *centralis* contains only cones and provides the sharpest vision.

*Aqueous* humour is produced by the *ciliary* *epithelium*, fills the anterior and posterior chambers, and drains via the *trabecular* meshwork into the canal of *Schlemm*. The *vitreous* humour fills the posterior segment and maintains the shape of the globe.
